strengths: Strong experience in modern full stack development (Java/Angular/React) indicating solid software engineering foundations.
Demonstrates practical project application of concepts like REST APIs, security (JWT), and database design.
Good professional structure with clear projects and skills listing.
weaknesses: Over-focus on high-level, recent technologies (Java Full Stack) while the JD requires core low-level languages (C/C++).
The resume does not explicitly showcase deep knowledge of Data Structures or Algorithms in a non-project context.
Experience seems heavily geared towards web application development rather than core systems programming.
recommendations: Crucially, add sections/bullet points to demonstrate proficiency and experience with C/C++. Since the JD is foundational (training phase), showcasing academic projects, competitive coding achievements, or specific coursework using C/C++ is essential.
Restructure the Technical Skills section to highlight 'Core Programming Fundamentals' (e.g., DSA implementation in C/C++) immediately after a brief summary.

When describing past projects, frame them by emphasizing algorithmic problem-solving aspects rather than just feature lists (e.g., instead of listing 'REST APIs,' describe optimizing the data flow using specific algorithms).
If possible, include achievements related to solving complex theoretical problems or contributing to C/C++ based systems/tools.
